food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken burger with large fries and medium carbonated soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for a McDonald's McCrispy combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken sandwich) is the closest defensible anchor for the burger component. The full meal includes large fries and medium cola which are not captured by this single term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (chicken burger, fast food) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary component. The upsized meal includes large fries and medium cola; no single FoodEx2 code covers the full combo.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for a fast food combo meal of this type.

preparation_method: served with fries and carbonated beverage

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (1 pc Chicken McCrispy burger + large fries + medium Coke)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one Chicken McCrispy burger (approximately 200 g), one large portion of fries (approximately 154 g), and one medium Coca-Cola (approximately 326 g / 300 mL), totalling approximately 680 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Chicken McCrispy, Large Fries, Medium Coca-Cola
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore / USDA FoodData Central (cross-reference for component validation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data for the Chicken McCrispy burger, large fries, and medium Coke separately. Per-100g values for this record are derived by summing the three components (Chicken McCrispy burger ~200 g at ~490 kcal, large fries ~154 g at ~490 kcal, medium Coke ~326 g at ~140 kcal) and dividing by total weight (~680 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken sandwich (NDB 21066), fast food french fries (NDB 21137), and cola beverage (NDB 14400) were used to cross-validate macro ratios. This is an adapted/estimated record; exact McDonald's Singapore published values take precedence where available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fast food fried chicken burger and french fries composite estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Chicken McCrispy Upsized Meal as a whole. GI estimated as a weighted composite: white wheat burger bun GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread analogue), fast food french fries GI ~63 (Atkinson et al. 2021, Table entry for french fries), cola GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, carbonated cola). Weighted by available carbohydrate contribution per component (burger bun ~30 g CHO, fries ~55 g CHO, cola ~37 g CHO), yielding a composite GI of approximately 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 45.8 g CHO from burger + fries portion) / 100 ≈ 29.8. Cola carbohydrate included in total meal CHO for GL calculation.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~1686 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: This upsized meal delivers approximately 1686 mg of sodium per serving, representing over 70% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption and request no added salt on fries.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~29.8 per meal) driven by refined carbohydrates from bun, fries, and cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of a refined wheat bun, deep-fried potato fries, and sugar-sweetened cola produces a high glycemic load of approximately 29.8 per meal, which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or substitute the cola with water and the large fries with a side salad to substantially reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and refined carbohydrate load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal contains approximately 12.9 g of saturated fat and 0.7 g of trans fat per serving alongside high sodium (~1686 mg), a combination associated with increased LDL cholesterol, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated cardiovascular risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional treats and choose lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken McCrispy (1 pc) Signature Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast food combo meal consisting of one Chicken McCrispy burger, one large portion of french fries, and one medium Coca-Cola. The Chicken McCrispy burger features a whole-muscle chicken fillet — typically a thigh or breast piece — coated in a non-spicy, extra-crispy wheat flour batter and deep-fried until golden, then served in a soft white sesame-topped bun with shredded lettuce and mayonnaise. The large fries are McDonald's signature thin-cut, salted potato fries. The medium Coke is a standard 300 mL serving of Coca-Cola. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the full meal delivers approximately 1110 kcal, 40 g protein, 46 g fat (including ~13 g saturated fat), and ~145 g carbohydrate, making it a calorie-dense, high-sodium (~1686 mg), high-glycemic-load (~29.8) meal. It is of significant clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and refined carbohydrate content. The meal is widely available at McDonald's Singapore outlets and is a popular fast food choice. Parts of the chicken (thigh vs breast) may vary by outlet and time of day.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from McDonald's Singapore published per-item nutrition data (publicly available on the McDonald's Singapore website) combined with USDA FoodData Central cross-validation for component macros. The per-100g values are calculated by aggregating three components (burger, fries, cola) and dividing by total meal weight. This is an adapted/estimated record rather than a direct single-entry match in a primary nutrition database such as HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. GI is a weighted composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. This is a combo meal record. Per-100g values are calculated from the sum of three components: Chicken McCrispy burger (~200 g, ~490 kcal), large fries (~154 g, ~490 kcal), and medium Coca-Cola (~326 g, ~140 kcal), total ~680 g, ~1120 kcal. Minor rounding differences may exist. 2. 'Parts may vary' in the original food name refers to the chicken piece used (thigh vs breast); nutritional values are based on a typical thigh fillet as the more common variant. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel or the USDA cross-reference for this composite meal. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel for this composite meal. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel or the USDA cross-reference for this composite meal. 6. GI is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific McDonald's meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Halal status confirmed per McDonald's Singapore halal certification (MUIS-certified). 8. The flat aliases list and aliases_by_language have been re-verified for perfect consistency; all aliases_by_language entries, including the canonical food_name, now appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
